title: Bank Reconciliation
event: Reyes Co.'s December 31 book balance of cash is $7,235; the bank statement shows $9,410. Reconciling items: deposits in transit $1,200, outstanding checks $2,150, a $1,650 note (including $50 interest) collected by the bank, a $45 bank service charge, and a $380 NSF check from a customer. Compute the adjusted cash balance.

computationPaths.narration: Two columns marching to the same number. Bank side gets the timing items the bank hasn't seen; book side gets the items the company learned from the statement.

memorize.body: Bank side = timing (Deposits in transit +, Outstanding checks −). Book side = news (notes collected +, charges −, NSF −).
The adjusted balance — not the bank's number, not the book's — is what the balance sheet reports.
DIT and outstanding checks NEVER touch the book side; the books already recorded them. Sorting items to the wrong column is the #1 exam error.

questions.prompt: Using the facts above, the adjusted cash balance = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the raw bank statement — before deposits in transit and outstanding checks. Adjusted cash is 8,460.
That's the unadjusted book balance — before the note, service charge, and NSF check the statement revealed. Adjusted cash is 8,460.
